vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The registry is largely non-representative of the current physical state. P1 (String of Nickels) is absent/bare soil. P2 (Mexican Mint) and P4 (Silver Guest) are not identifiable as distinct specimens. P3 (Pothos) is represented only by the anchor (white rabbit) and a single, struggling sprout.

inventory_reconciliation.p1: Systemic Loss: No visible plant matter.
inventory_reconciliation.p2: Systemic Loss: No visible plant matter.
inventory_reconciliation.p3: Partial Presence: White rabbit anchor present; single sprout observed.
inventory_reconciliation.p4: Systemic Loss: No visible plant matter.

plant_audit.p1_yellow_pot: Empty soil surface; no signs of String of Nickels.
plant_audit.p2_black_pot: Empty soil surface; no signs of Mexican Mint.
plant_audit.p3_black_pot: Contains white rabbit anchor and one green, emerging sprout.
plant_audit.p4_shared_black_pot: Empty soil surface; no signs of Silver Guest.

biome_observations.soil_texture: Consistently dark and damp across all pots; no visible fungal blooms or crusting.
biome_observations.incidental_growth: One singular, uncatalogued green sprout in P3.
biome_observations.desk_surface: Clean, no significant debris or anomalies.

temporal_deltas.earliest_to_t3: No significant change; pots remain largely barren.
temporal_deltas.t3_to_t2: Sprout in P3 becomes slightly more defined/upright.
temporal_deltas.t2_to_t1: Sprout in P3 shows minimal growth; no other changes.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is in a state of near-total systemic failure. The only sign of life is a single sprout in P3. The lack of foliage in P1, P2, and P4 suggests either total senescence or failure to germinate/establish.
anomalies: Presence of a single, uncatalogued sprout in P3.
Total absence of expected botanical specimens in P1, P2, and P4.
vision_report.narrative_description: I have performed a chronological audit of the provided image sequence. The biome is currently experiencing a severe lack of botanical occupancy. The registry provided does not match the visual evidence, as three of the four pots are devoid of plant life. The only active biological element is a single, isolated sprout in the P3 pot, which is currently being monitored alongside the white rabbit anchor. The soil appears consistently damp, yet there is no evidence of the expected species, indicating a potential failure in the initial planting or a catastrophic loss of specimens prior to the start of this sequence.
